full_text:
- Uttarakhand Weather Update: Plains Cross 40°C, Heatwave Intensifies; Rain, Thunder Alert In Hill Districts
Uttarakhand scorches past 40°C in plains, heatwave intensifies:IMD issues rain, thunder alert in hills, relief in sight
- Copy link
Temperatures in parts of Uttarakhand have crossed the 40°C mark, with Haridwar, Rudrapur and Kashipur among the worst affected. Even as heat intensifies in the plains, the India Meteorological Department has issued an alert for rain and thunderstorms in five hilly districts.
According to the Meteorological Centre in Dehradun, light to moderate rainfall is likely at isolated places in Uttarkashi, Rudraprayag, Chamoli, Bageshwar and Pithoragarh. Other hill districts may also witness cloudy skies, light showers and thunderstorms, which could bring down temperatures and offer some relief.
PHOTOS related to weather
Plains heat wave persists, temperatures set to rise further
Dry conditions will continue across the plains of Uttarakhand, including Rudrapur, Kashipur, Haridwar and Haldwani, with no immediate relief from the heat. Intense sunshine is expected to push temperatures up by another 2–3°C.
The India Meteorological Department has also issued a warning for lightning and gusty winds reaching speeds of 40–50 kmph in isolated areas.
Dehradun to remain dry, heat to persist
The state capital Dehradun will see clear skies and dry weather through the day. The maximum temperature is likely to hover around 36°C, while the minimum may settle near 20°C. Heat conditions will prevail, prompting authorities to advise residents to avoid unnecessary exposure during the afternoon, stay well-hydrated and use adequate sun protection.
Chardham route to witness shifting weather conditions
Weather along the Chardham pilgrimage route is expected to remain variable. While the plains continue to face intense heat, higher-altitude regions are likely to experience cooler and unsettled conditions.
Areas around Kedarnath, Badrinath, Gangotri and Yamunotri are expected to remain cloudy, with chances of light rain at a few locations. Higher reaches may also receive light snowfall, leading to a noticeable drop in temperatures.
Temperatures across the Chardham region are likely to stay below normal, with cold conditions persisting—particularly during the morning and evening hours.
Temperature of major cities of the state
Outlook for next three days
April 26: Light rain and thunderstorms likely in hilly districts; plains to remain hot and dry.
April 27: Similar pattern to continue; isolated rain in hills, high temperatures in plains.
April 28: Light to moderate rain and thunderstorms expected in Uttarkashi, Rudraprayag, Chamoli, Bageshwar and Pithoragarh; partial cloud cover likely in plains.
Overall, Uttarakhand is witnessing a sharp contrast in weather, with scorching heat in the plains and cooler, unstable conditions in the hills.
